title: The Headless Gendou
wikitext:
[]: The Headless Gendou             Quote Post by unitM » Fri Jan 24, 2014 4:48 pm There is a number of themes associated with headlessness in NTE. I want to see what people think of it all because there's enough symbology throughout 3 anyways. Starting with the lighter stuff , which may not necessarily be connected to the headless-ness that Q takes on in several ways, Lilith's mask is different throughout NTE and is physically screwed onto Lilith, taking the place of the more natural black moon-textured SEELE mask that was in NGE The black moon-textured SEELE mask is seen being attached to Mark 06(Mark 06 is later beheaded in the activation of Unit 13) There are various unexplained helmets, visors, and eyepatches in NTE now, which were not around prior to the theatrical edition Unit 01, in a state of dummy plug automation, bites through Asuka's entry plug, whereas in NGE the dummy plug from this fight was simply crushed in 01's hands Unit 13 chomps the shrunken-down angel from 3 Unit 08 was seen with a halo in the preview for Q after 2, but the strange thing is, the halo was not displayed over 08's head but rather under the Evangelion itself (debatable) All the pilots in 3 under Gendou's authority are wearing plugsuits that have a red collar and are darker coloured from the neck down, thus accentuating the head of the pilots Now onto the stuff that will really expand your mind, In End of Evangelion, giant Rei's head is the first thing that is toppled off and remains preserved on earth, even after Shinji's trip The smuggled Nebuchadnezzar's Key from 2 is displayed as a small human nervous system right up to the neck - there is no head present [s]Shinji is the only character to have his neck prominently exposed throughout 3[/s] Rei Q operates a headless Mark 09 Evangelion unit Mark 09 is an Evangelion unit most noticably equipped with a scythe, a weapon associated commonly with beheadings Rei Q, the person, does not read or is unable to communicate properly - her Evangelion is not the only thing that is effectively missing it's head Seemingly outside of Rei Q's control, Mark 09 regenerates it's missing head in the shape of the Wunder's exterior bridge, just before firing a an effective laser attack Upon Mark 09's head regeneration, Rei regains some form of self-control( see 2 points above point ) Shinji's fainting in 3 is associated with not only the head rush he receives when Rei tells Shinji that she is unsure if he saved her but also the number of other powerful and confusing words that are voicing themselves through Shinji's head We're primarily shown this fainting scene through Shinji's facial and head region The DSS choker Out of frustration over Kaworu persuading Shinji to pilot 13 with him, Shinji bashes his head into his bedside wall Fuyutsuki refers to Unit 13's birthing sequence as the final executor The failures of infinity in the central dogma have no heads There is a gigantic face - a head - dug deep into the planet Shinji's SDAT player is essentially a device that allows Shinji to escape his current physical reality into his own headspace Shinji's SDAT player is broken in 3, which Kaworu promptly repairs, but Shinji no longer has the desire to listen to it at the end of the movie The SDAT player itself is carried by Rei in 2 and Shinji in 3, though it is unknown whether either of them bring it along at the end of 3 Lilith is headless Gendou is in control of a bulky severed head that resembles Yuis or Reis but likely was once attached to the headless Lilith When Shinji scales Lilith and locks out Kaworu, Unit 13's eyes glow from a colour-coordinated yellow to a strange red The SEELE monoliths each have detailed images of brains on them Mark 06 is fused with Lilith where Lilith's head should be Mark 09, piloted by Rei though ordered by someone else, lops off Mark 06's head with her scythe The angel inhabiting Mark 06 wraps around Unit 13 to form at first a giant Rei head Inside the plug of Unit 13, during the ceremony where it is inside the hug of the angel( see above 2 points ), there are small Rei heads that can be seen popping out of the LCL When Unit 13 is officially activated, one of the first things we are shown is Unit 13's eyes blowing up and popping out, as if Unit 13 is suffering an extreme head trip(which it likely is, due to activation) At first I was a little hesitant to compile this list. The themes of the headless Mark 09 and the headless Lilith at first were my biggest temptations, but when I had a closer look at the Key of Nezboogers, I decided to. The theme of headless-ness has actually been seen a lot throughout literature. In Alice's Adventures in Wonderland, for example, many critics attest most of the book and Alice's adventures to a literal head trip. The Queen of Hearts famously shouts at the top of her lungs "Off with her head!" Though I am not suggesting Shinji is on drugs, though it is certainly a possibility, it is undoubtedly a "head trip" for Shinji to live in a world which is almost entirely foreign to him. What does any of this mean? Why is there so much head loss? How come my avatar on this forum still isn't showing up? I don't know if any of these have answers but it is worth the discussion.

[]: Re: The Headless Gendou             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Jan 24, 2014 5:48 pm unitM wrote: How come my avatar on this forum still isn't showing up?

[]: Re: The Headless Gendou             Quote Post by unitM » Fri Jan 24, 2014 6:25 pm pwhodges wrote: Have you confused "user image", which is just part of the profile, with "avatar" which you have to scroll down further to find? I did at first, and looking at your user image, I guess you did too. As for headlessness - I suspect that some of your selections are too tenuous, and so the whole will add up to less than you hope.

[]: Re: The Headless Gendou             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jan 24, 2014 7:03 pm unitM wrote: Shinji is the only character to have his neck prominently exposed throughout 3

[]: Re: The Headless Gendou             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jan 24, 2014 9:55 pm unitM wrote: [*]Unit 08 was seen with a halo in the preview for Q after 2, but the strange thing is, the halo was not displayed over 08's head but rather under the Evangelion itself

[]: Re: The Headless Gendou             Quote Post by unitM » Sat Jan 25, 2014 12:05 am @SSD The halo thing was a minor point. But even so, I would not be surprised if the rituals were a little... different for containing and controlling an angel. I mean, even discarding the position of the halo on 08, the Eva itself is in a very peculiar position. Monk Ed wrote: How so? And why not Kaworu too? Kaworu wears the choker too.

[]:             Quote Post by Kid » Mon Jan 27, 2014 2:08 pm Wow! I've been working on a modified version of an old theory but I just couldn't find one unifying detail regarding headlessness/decapitation and you've helped me out immensely by pointing out that the Key of Nebuchadnezzar is headless. You've done good.

[]: Re: The Headless Gendou             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Jan 27, 2014 2:31 pm unitM wrote: @SSD The halo thing was a minor point. But even so, I would not be surprised if the rituals were a little... different for containing and controlling an angel. I mean, even discarding the position of the halo on 08, the Eva itself is in a very peculiar position.

Starting with the lighter stuff , which may not necessarily be connected to the headless-ness that Q takes on in several ways, Lilith's mask is different throughout NTE and is physically screwed onto Lilith, taking the place of the more natural black moon-textured SEELE mask that was in NGE The black moon-textured SEELE mask is seen being attached to Mark 06(Mark 06 is later beheaded in the activation of Unit 13) There are various unexplained helmets, visors, and eyepatches in NTE now, which were not around prior to the theatrical edition Unit 01, in a state of dummy plug automation, bites through Asuka's entry plug, whereas in NGE the dummy plug from this fight was simply crushed in 01's hands Unit 13 chomps the shrunken-down angel from 3 Unit 08 was seen with a halo in the preview for Q after 2, but the strange thing is, the halo was not displayed over 08's head but rather under the Evangelion itself (debatable) All the pilots in 3 under Gendou's authority are wearing plugsuits that have a red collar and are darker coloured from the neck down, thus accentuating the head of the pilots Now onto the stuff that will really expand your mind, In End of Evangelion, giant Rei's head is the first thing that is toppled off and remains preserved on earth, even after Shinji's trip The smuggled Nebuchadnezzar's Key from 2 is displayed as a small human nervous system right up to the neck - []: Re: The Headless Gendou             Quote Post by unitM » Mon Feb 03, 2014 4:39 pm ElMariachi wrote: Notes that a similar halo is present under Gendo when he's floating during his farewell speech to SEELE : [wkimg]File:Eva3-33 C1288 A Gendo Float Monoliths.jpg[/wkimg] So my guess is that these purple halos are some sort of antigrav technology to keep something(or someone) floating at a desired height. Since in the shot of EVA-08, it was still under construction, the more probable was that it was kept in this position to install some components or something like that.

Article: The Headless Gendou
[]: The Headless Gendou             Quote Post by unitM » Fri Jan 24, 2014 4:48 pm There is a number of themes associated with headlessness in NTE. I want to see what people think of it all because there's enough symbology throughout 3 anyways. Starting with the lighter stuff , which may not necessarily be connected to the headless-ness that Q takes on in several ways, Lilith's mask is different throughout NTE and is physically screwed onto Lilith, taking the place of the more natural black moon-textured SEELE mask that was in NGE The black moon-textured SEELE mask is seen being attached to Mark 06(Mark 06 is later beheaded in the activation of Unit 13) There are various unexplained helmets, visors, and eyepatches in NTE now, which were not around prior to the theatrical edition Unit 01, in a state of dummy plug automation, bites through Asuka's entry plug, whereas in NGE the dummy plug from this fight was simply crushed in 01's hands Unit 13 chomps the shrunken-down angel from 3 Unit 08 was seen with a halo in the preview for Q after 2, but the strange thing is, the halo was not displayed over 08's head but rather under the Evangelion itself (debatable) All the pilots in 3 under Gendou's authority are wearing plugsuits that have a red collar and are darker coloured from the neck down, thus accentuating the head of the pilots Now onto the stuff that will really expand your mind, In End of Evangelion, giant Rei's head is the first thing that is toppled off and remains preserved on earth, even after Shinji's trip The smuggled Nebuchadnezzar's Key from 2 is displayed as a small human nervous system right up to the neck - there is no head present [s]Shinji is the only character to have his neck prominently exposed throughout 3[/s] Rei Q operates a headless Mark 09 Evangelion unit Mark 09 is an Evangelion unit most noticably equipped with a scythe, a weapon associated commonly with beheadings Rei Q, the person, does not read or is unable to communicate properly - her Evangelion is not the only thing that is effectively missing it's head Seemingly outside of Rei Q's control, Mark 09 regenerates it's missing head in the

Article: The Headless Gendou
 the person, does not read or is unable to communicate properly - her Evangelion is not the only thing that is effectively missing it's head Seemingly outside of Rei Q's control, Mark 09 regenerates it's missing head in the shape of the Wunder's exterior bridge, just before firing a an effective laser attack Upon Mark 09's head regeneration, Rei regains some form of self-control( see 2 points above point ) Shinji's fainting in 3 is associated with not only the head rush he receives when Rei tells Shinji that she is unsure if he saved her but also the number of other powerful and confusing words that are voicing themselves through Shinji's head We're primarily shown this fainting scene through Shinji's facial and head region The DSS choker Out of frustration over Kaworu persuading Shinji to pilot 13 with him, Shinji bashes his head into his bedside wall Fuyutsuki refers to Unit 13's birthing sequence as the final executor The failures of infinity in the central dogma have no heads There is a gigantic face - a head - dug deep into the planet Shinji's SDAT player is essentially a device that allows Shinji to escape his current physical reality into his own headspace Shinji's SDAT player is broken in 3, which Kaworu promptly repairs, but Shinji no longer has the desire to listen to it at the end of the movie The SDAT player itself is carried by Rei in 2 and Shinji in 3, though it is unknown whether either of them bring it along at the end of 3 Lilith is headless Gendou is in control of a bulky severed head that resembles Yuis or Reis but likely was once attached to the headless Lilith When Shinji scales Lilith and locks out Kaworu, Unit 13's eyes glow from a colour-coordinated yellow to a strange red The SEELE monoliths each have detailed images of brains on them Mark 06 is fused with Lilith where Lilith's head should be Mark 09, piloted by Rei though ordered by someone else, lops off Mark 06's head with her scythe The angel inhabiting Mark 06 wraps around Unit 13 to form at first a giant Rei head Inside the plug of Unit 13, during the ceremony where it is inside the hug of the angel( see above 2 points ), there are small Rei heads that can be seen popping out of the LCL When Unit

Article: The Headless Gendou
 first a giant Rei head Inside the plug of Unit 13, during the ceremony where it is inside the hug of the angel( see above 2 points ), there are small Rei heads that can be seen popping out of the LCL When Unit 13 is officially activated, one of the first things we are shown is Unit 13's eyes blowing up and popping out, as if Unit 13 is suffering an extreme head trip(which it likely is, due to activation) At first I was a little hesitant to compile this list. The themes of the headless Mark 09 and the headless Lilith at first were my biggest temptations, but when I had a closer look at the Key of Nezboogers, I decided to. The theme of headless-ness has actually been seen a lot throughout literature. In Alice's Adventures in Wonderland, for example, many critics attest most of the book and Alice's adventures to a literal head trip. The Queen of Hearts famously shouts at the top of her lungs "Off with her head!" Though I am not suggesting Shinji is on drugs, though it is certainly a possibility, it is undoubtedly a "head trip" for Shinji to live in a world which is almost entirely foreign to him. What does any of this mean? Why is there so much head loss? How come my avatar on this forum still isn't showing up? I don't know if any of these have answers but it is worth the discussion.
